reasoning:
Evidence up to November 2025 indicates no Chinese military base in a BRICS country has been publicly announced by either the Chinese government or the US intelligence community. Cambodia (Ream Naval Base) is routinely cited as the most advanced new Chinese overseas military outpost, but Cambodia is not a BRICS member. The UAE has been flagged in leaked US intelligence in 2023 and the 2024 US DOD report on China's military power, but the DOD report only categorized the UAE as a location where China "probably has considered" establishing "military logistics facilities," which falls short of a public announcement of an existing or under-construction military base. 

Given the short time window remaining before the resolution date (Jan 1, 2026) and the high geopolitical sensitivity that typically delays or obscures such announcements, the likelihood of a major base announcement in the next 50 days is extremely low. The community prediction was at ~8% in late October; without any recent triggering events, I am dropping the estimate to 4% since time is running out.
